hard_constraints: READ ONLY. Do not click Edit-then-Save, Create, Delete, Pause, or any confirm/submit control.
Opening an audience to view its details is fine; do NOT save or change anything.
Do not modify any ad set, ad, or campaign.
If any action would change state, stop and record it in notes instead of doing it.
name: audience-audit

output.primary: Write results as JSON to the Drive Working Folder as audience-audit__result.json
output.secondary_preferred: If repo write is available, also commit the same JSON to hosting/audience-audit-result.json so it is fetchable via raw.githubusercontent.com

purpose: Read Meta custom audiences to verify retention windows and detect duplicates for the retargeting funnel audit. Observe and report only.
tasks: 1. On the Audiences page, capture the full list of custom audiences. For each row record: name, audience ID, type (Website/Customer list/Lookalike/Engagement), size/availability, and date created.

2. Flag any two or more audiences that look like duplicates or near-duplicates of each other (same type, similar name, similar rule, e.g. two 'all visitors' website audiences). List them explicitly as suspected duplicates with both IDs.

3. Open the audience named 'Website Visitors 30d - excl purchasers' (ID 120248149564140706). Read and record its retention window in days (the 'in the past N days' value), and whether it excludes purchasers (any exclusion referencing a Purchase event). Record the exact include and exclude rules shown.
4. If an audience with ID ending 458390706 exists, open it and record the same details (name, retention days, include/exclude rules). If no such audience exists, record that explicitly.

5. For the two ad sets 'Warm - All Visitors 90d' (120250419038510706) and 'Warm - All Visitors 30d' (120248158412480706): record which custom audience each one targets (name + ID) and whether each ad set is currently Active or Off. Do not change either.
6. Note any audience showing an error, 'too small', 'populating', or unavailable-for-delivery status.
